entities.name: Seed-stage startups
entities.context: Early-stage companies characterized by high entropy, lack of structure, and systemic support

entities.name: Series A/B stages
entities.context: Later-stage startups offering superior balance of risk, foundational support, and mental stability

entities.name: Rational Choice Theory
entities.context: Decision-making framework that V argues must account for psychological capital, not just financial equity

entities.name: psychological capital
entities.context: Mental and emotional resources that factor into career transition decisions

entities.name: financial equity
entities.context: Ownership stake in startups, often offered as compensation for risk

entities.name: chaos tolerance
entities.context: Psychological trait determining suitability for high-entropy startup environments; proposed screening criterion

entities.name: founder-lite psychological profile
entities.context: Personality type required to thrive in Seed-stage chaos without systemic support

entities.name: mid-career specialists
entities.context: Professionals who derive value from mastery and structured execution

entities.name: talent matching
entities.context: Process of placing candidates in appropriate company stages based on psychological fit

relationships.context: Seed stages impose psychological costs that act as a tax on productivity and well-being
Recommended as rational choice for specialists due to existing foundation and structure
V argues the theory must expand to account for psychological factors beyond financial equity
Seed environments require this specific profile to offset lack of structure
Specialists should be screened for chaos tolerance before Seed placement
Skills can be better leveraged where foundational support exists
